---
title: "Black Screen after NVIDIA Driver Install"
date: 2011-04-20
forum: General Help
---

### Post by J4x0 on 2011-04-20
I'm trying to play games on my ubuntu desktop.
So I installed the NVIDIA driver by some commands:
```
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install nvidia-current
[COLOR=Red]-reboot-[/COLOR]
sudo nvidia-xconfig
```Now when I boot my PC, I get a black screen.
My monitor tries to find a signal by switching from digital to analog.
But I can hear the ubuntu sound and when I press CTRL+ALT+F1 I get the tty1.

Xorg.conf:
```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 260.19.06  (buildmeister@builder101)  Mon Sep 13 07:06:38 PDT 2010


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 33.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection
```Xorg.0.log:

```Can someone help me please?

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-04-20
Choose recovery mode (or fallback or whatever it's called from grub) so that you can reach a console. Then delete your xorg.conf
```
sudo rm /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
and reboot
```
sudo reboot
```

Once (if) you get into your Desktop, open a terminal and give
```
gksu nvidia-settings
```
Do your adjustments from there, click "Save to X configuration file" and reboot.
Post back with the outcome.

---

### Post by J4x0 on 2011-04-20
Thanks for the fast reply.
After deleting the xorg.conf I could manage to login without getting a black screen.
But when I open the NVIDIA X Server Settings by the command I get this:
[IMG]http://i.imgur.com/xF2X2.png[/IMG]

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-04-20
Instead of installing the nvidia driver by command line, have tried from Administration>System>Hardware drivers?

---

### Post by J4x0 on 2011-04-20
I did before but got the same issue.
- No ubuntu logo just "Ubuntu 10.10"
- Black screen 
:(
After i messed something up with Ubuntu i did a restall.
Then I googled for another method and saw this method so I decided to give it a try.

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-04-20
Ok then...
Give
```
sudo nvidia-xconfig
```
in terminal but don't reboot.
Run
```
gksu nvidia-settings 
```
make sure all your tweaks are ok, save to X conf file, reboot and post back.

---

### Post by J4x0 on 2011-04-20
Same error again when opening nvidia-settings :(

---

### Post by J4x0 on 2011-04-20
Its fixed!
A friend gave me this link: [http://www.nvidia.com/object/linux-display-amd64-270.41.06-driver.html](http://www.nvidia.com/object/linux-display-amd64-270.41.06-driver.html)
It works now thanks

---
